topic: Someone discovers their friend has been lying about their job
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox like a physical blow. It was a formal notification from OmniCorp, detailing a recent internal audit and, more damningly, a discrepancy in my employment verification. My stomach clenched, a tight, cold knot forming beneath my ribs. I reread the text three times, the words blurring into an incomprehensible mess of corporate jargon. It was all tied back to Liam. Liam, my best friend, the guy who always boasted about his prestigious position in software development. The realization hit me with sickening force: he hadn't been working at the firm he claimed to be. My hands started shaking so badly I had to grip the edge of my desk until my knuckles were white. I stared at the screen, the fluorescent lights of my apartment suddenly seeming too bright, too harsh. When Liam finally called, his voice sounded strained, overly bright. "Hey, what's up?" I managed, my voice coming out thin and reedy. "Nothing," I lied, the sound of my own deception echoing in the silence. I just wanted the floor to swallow me whole.

[story 2]

I found out by accident, sifting through some old LinkedIn recommendations I hadn't looked at in years. The details were so precise, the dates so specific, that a wave of nausea washed over me. It was Liam. The picture he’d curated for years, the one showing him in a sharp suit in front of a gleaming skyscraper—it was all a fabrication. I felt a sudden, intense heat rising up my neck, flushing my entire face. Every casual greeting from Liam now felt like a deliberate, calculated trap. When he finally confessed, stammering and avoiding eye contact, the air in the coffee shop thickened instantly. I gripped my mug so tightly the ceramic threatened to crack. I couldn't meet his gaze; I focused instead on the swirling pattern in the tabletop, trying desperately to keep my breathing even. "Liam," I said, my tone carefully neutral, though the tremor in my voice betrayed the turmoil churning inside me. "That’s… a lot to process."

[story 3]

The proof wasn't subtle. It was a leaked internal memo I stumbled upon while searching for a shared project file—a memo detailing a restructuring that clearly hadn't happened at the company Liam told everyone he worked for. The world seemed to tilt on its axis. I sat on the edge of my bed, the floorboards cool against my bare legs, unable to move. My vision tunneled slightly. It wasn't just that he was dishonest; it was the sheer, elaborate performance he’d put on for everyone, including me. A profound sense of awkwardness settled over me, heavy and suffocating. I finally gathered the courage to confront him. When he saw the look on my face—the wide, unblinking stare—he visibly deflated. "Look, I messed up," he mumbled, avoiding my eyes entirely. I just nodded slowly, unable to form coherent words, feeling a strange, heavy weight pressing down on my chest. It felt like every piece of my carefully constructed composure had just shattered into dust.
